channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Double Fine petitions to unionize
item.content:encoded:
Via Double Fine/Microsoft

Psychonauts and recently released Kiln studio Double Fine is set to unionize, per a petition with the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) filed May 7 (thanks, Aftermath). The Microsoft-owned studio is the latest to take the union step, in conjunction with Communication Workers of America (CWA), and the petition notes that all "regular part-time and full-time employees" (around 42) are included.

"On May 7, the workers at Microsoft studio Double Fine Productions announced their decision to form a union with CWA to preserve and extend the studio’s commitments to creative excellence, diversity and inclusion, and worker quality of life," CWA told Aftermath. 

"In tandem with requesting voluntary recognition from the company, workers have also filed an election petition with the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to secure union representation. We appreciate that Microsoft has taken a neutral approach and agreed not to interfere in any way with workers' rights to organize unions."

Related:PlayStation sees AI as a 'powerful tool,' teams including Naughty Dog, San Diego Studio already using it

Double Fine is the latest under the Microsoft banner to unionize, following in the footsteps of id Software last December and workers on the Diablo series last August.

Microsoft, for its part, has laid off thousands of workers in the last few years since its titanic merger with Activision Blizzard. More recently, there have been a number of executive shake-ups at the top of the org chart, alongside price cuts for Game Pass and nixing a Copilot AI initiative.

About the Author

Editor-in-Chief, GameDeveloper.com

Danielle is the editor-in-chief of Game Developer, with previous editorial posts at Fanbyte, VICE, and Polygon. She’s also a lecturer in game design at the Berklee College of Music, and a hobbyist game developer in her spare time.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/double-fine-petitions-to-unionize
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
